added_assumptions.label: A-cost-separation
added_assumptions.statement: The drift cost c: U × U → ℝ_{≥0} (ass:drift) is positive off the diagonal: c(u,v) = 0 if and only if u = v, for all u,v ∈ U.
added_assumptions.source: Standard nondegeneracy condition for a transport / drift cost function. The reviewer notes that ass:drift currently only asserts c ≥ 0, which permits zero-cost off-diagonal transitions and breaks the ρ = 0 ⇒ no-drift step in prop:point-collapse. Making c separate points is the minimal generic fix; it is not the kernel claim of the proposal (the kernel is the observed-increment strict-tightening characterization).

flags.rewound_from_stage0: Case 2b (Bucket A, caveat-class cost separation) + Case 6 partial (novelty floor missed because the derivation collapsed to generic finite-LP/support-function content rather than proving the promised observed-increment strict-tightening kernel). The conjecture is salvageable — reviewer explicitly lists viable field-tier deliverables (strict-tightening criterion in the minimal binary noisy-panel model, closed-form sharp transport bound, or sharp counterexample); the failure is at the derivation layer, not the conjecture. Per-boundary heuristic suggested stage_0 over user; overriding the alternative stage_neg1 because the reviewer did not flag the conjecture as counterexample-grade.

proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of long-term ATE when the surrogate-index assumption fails through unobserved post-surrogate drift, using multiple noisy surrogates and a short experimental panel
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art is ThmSmith/doc/API.md Section 3, which records PartialID as an empty seed catalogue, plus archived same-qid angles 0 and 1. Angle 0 was generic noisy-panel drift LP tightening and angle 1 was common-kernel noisy-proxy bridge compatibility. This proposal targets a different kernel: a HuangWangYuanZhaoZhang2026 longitudinal surrogate/pivot-index failure mode where bounded post-surrogate binary drift dilates terminal-prevalence intervals and yields a point-ID phase diagram.","published_axis":"Closest published papers are HuangWangYuanZhaoZhang2026 on longitudinal surrogate/pivot-index point extrapolation, AtheyChettyImbensKang2019 on surrogate-index point identification, FanManzanaresParkQi2026 on terminal-copula sensitivity sets, HungChen2026 on proximal surrogate-index point identification, and ChenGengJia2007/YinLiuGengLuo2020 on surrogate-paradox sign criteria. The gap is a closed-form sharp bounded-drift phase diagram for the post-surrogate latent transition, not another point estimator, terminal copula bound, or qualitative paradox criterion."}
seed_list: Prior work AtheyChettyImbensKang2019 would imply that long-term ATE is point identified when the surrogate index spans the treatment-outcome path. Our seed conjectures that bounded post-surrogate drift leaves a nondegenerate but sharply characterizable interval, and multiple noisy surrogate waves strictly tighten that interval only through panel-increment compatibility.

Prior work FanManzanaresParkQi2026 would imply that unknown conditional copulas around terminal surrogate indices organize the relevant partial-ID set. Our seed conjectures that terminal-only copula bounds are nonsharp when noisy preterminal surrogate increments restrict latent drift paths that the terminal copula leaves free.

Prior work HuangWangYuanZhaoZhang2026 would predict that longitudinal surrogate/pivot-index assumptions can extrapolate long-term treatment effects from short experimental panels. Our seed conjectures that when longitudinal surrogacy fails through unobserved drift, the surviving object is a transport-radius bound with a sharp point-ID collapse only at zero drift.

Prior work LalImbensHull2026 would imply that many noisy proxies can reduce bias by recovering low-dimensional latent surrogates. Our seed conjectures that under overlapping measurement error and post-surrogate drift, any finite proxy panel can still leave sign ambiguity unless a computable surrogate-index margin exceeds the sharp residual drift radius.

Prior work BibautKallusEjdemyrZhao2023 would imply that imperfect surrogates can be handled using many weak experiments and proxy moments. Our seed conjectures that one randomized short panel can sometimes detect and bound leakage without many weak experiments because treatment changes in surrogate increments impose response-type compatibility constraints.

Prior work Prentice1989 would predict validity only when the surrogate history captures the treatment-outcome association. Our seed conjectures that a failed Prentice condition caused by bounded post-surrogate drift admits a sharp partial-ID replacement rather than an all-or-nothing validity failure.

Prior work FrangakisRubin2002 would imply that post-treatment surrogate reasoning should be organized by principal strata. Our seed conjectures that multiple noisy surrogate measurements shrink the relevant principal-stratum mixture only through a finite measurement-compatibility polytope, producing a strict bound gap from terminal-stratum analyses.

Prior work ChenGengJia2007 would warn that positive surrogate effects can coexist with negative endpoint effects. Our seed conjectures a sharp sign-identification boundary: the surrogate-index margin dominates all feasible drift paths if and only if the long-term ATE sign is identified.

Prior work LiLuedtke2023 would imply that efficient fusion of data sources is central once surrogate information is available. Our seed conjectures that under post-surrogate drift the efficiency question is secondary to a nondegenerate identified interval whose width is governed by drift and noisy-panel compatibility.

Prior work ChenRitzwoller2023 would imply that semiparametric long-term treatment-effect estimation is feasible by combining short-term experimental and long-term observational samples. Our seed conjectures that if the observational bridge only identifies a no-drift response curve, then treated post-surrogate drift forces sharp bounds rather than a regular point estimand.

Prior work Manski1990 would imply bounded-outcome intervals when the long-term outcome is unobserved. Our seed conjectures that noisy surrogate panels and drift budgets generate a strict refinement of the Manski interval through finite response-type restrictions.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AtheyChettyImbensKang2019 would imply that long-term ATE is point identified when the surrogate index spans the treatment-outcome path. Our seed conjectures that bounded post-surrogate drift leaves a nondegenerate but sharply characterizable interval, and multiple noisy surrogate waves strictly tighten that interval only through panel-increment compatibility.

anchor_paper.author: Athey, Chetty, Imbens, Kang
anchor_paper.venue: NBER Working Paper 26463; Review of Economic Studies advance version
anchor_paper.claim: Under Prentice surrogacy and comparability, the average effect on the surrogate index equals the long-term ATE.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not a generic assumption weakening; the proposed mechanism is a post-surrogate drift state that creates a nondegenerate sharp interval and a strict panel-vs-terminal information gap.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from AtheyChettyImbensKang2019 because their bias formula already covers failures of surrogacy.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their bias discussion is not a sharp finite identified set with noisy repeated surrogates and bounded latent drift.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work FanManzanaresParkQi2026 would imply that unknown conditional copulas around terminal surrogate indices organize the relevant partial-ID set. Our seed conjectures that terminal-only copula bounds are nonsharp when noisy preterminal surrogate increments restrict latent drift paths that the terminal copula leaves free.

anchor_paper.author: Fan, Manzanares, Park, Qi
anchor_paper.claim: Unknown conditional copulas yield identified sets for weighted surrogate-index ATEs.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: this is not adding another surrogate; it predicts strict nonsharpness of terminal-only copula bounds from panel-increment constraints.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from FanManzanaresParkQi2026 because their unknown-copula set is already the identified set.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their object conditions on terminal observed surrogates; it does not use preterminal noisy increments to constrain latent drift histories.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work HuangWangYuanZhaoZhang2026 would predict that longitudinal surrogate/pivot-index assumptions can extrapolate long-term treatment effects from short experimental panels. Our seed conjectures that when longitudinal surrogacy fails through unobserved drift, the surviving object is a transport-radius bound with a sharp point-ID collapse only at zero drift.

anchor_paper.author: Huang, Wang, Yuan, Zhao, Zhang
anchor_paper.claim: Longitudinal surrogate and pivot-index assumptions identify long-term treatment effects from short experimental data.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not just weakening longitudinal surrogacy; the drift budget creates a phase boundary between point ID and interval ID.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the Management Science framework by relaxing their assumptions.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their framework targets point extrapolation; it does not give a sharp transport-style partial-ID boundary when the pivot condition fails.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work LalImbensHull2026 would imply that many noisy proxies can reduce bias by recovering low-dimensional latent surrogates. Our seed conjectures that under overlapping measurement error and post-surrogate drift, any finite proxy panel can still leave sign ambiguity unless a computable surrogate-index margin exceeds the sharp residual drift radius.

anchor_paper.author: Lal, Imbens, Hull
anchor_paper.claim: Many noisy proxies can improve long-term causal inference by recovering low-dimensional latent surrogates.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not a many-proxy extension; it identifies a sign-ambiguity threshold that survives finite noisy proxy panels.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from measurement-error attenuation arguments in LalImbensHull2026.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their bias-variance analysis is not a sharp partial-ID sign theorem under adversarial post-surrogate drift.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BibautKallusEjdemyrZhao2023 would imply that imperfect surrogates can be handled using many weak experiments and proxy moments. Our seed conjectures that one randomized short panel can sometimes detect and bound leakage without many weak experiments because treatment changes in surrogate increments impose response-type compatibility constraints.

anchor_paper.author: Bibaut, Kallus, Ejdemyr, Zhao
anchor_paper.claim: Many weak experiments and proxy moments can handle imperfect surrogates and effect leakage.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the phenomenon is that panel increments substitute for weak-experiment variation only through a sharp compatibility constraint.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from their proxy-moment framework because panel increments are just more proxies.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed result is about one-experiment sharp identification under drift, not asymptotic proxy moments across many weak experiments.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Prentice1989 would predict validity only when the surrogate history captures the treatment-outcome association. Our seed conjectures that a failed Prentice condition caused by bounded post-surrogate drift admits a sharp partial-ID replacement rather than an all-or-nothing validity failure.

anchor_paper.author: Prentice
anchor_paper.claim: A valid surrogate endpoint must operationally capture the treatment effect on the true endpoint.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not merely weakening Prentice; it replaces validity with a finite sharp identified set under a named dynamic failure mode.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from general bounds once Prentice fails.
seed_details.rebuttal: General bounds do not use noisy panel measurement and bounded post-surrogate drift restrictions.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work FrangakisRubin2002 would imply that post-treatment surrogate reasoning should be organized by principal strata. Our seed conjectures that multiple noisy surrogate measurements shrink the relevant principal-stratum mixture only through a finite measurement-compatibility polytope, producing a strict bound gap from terminal-stratum analyses.

anchor_paper.author: Frangakis, Rubin
anchor_paper.claim: Principal stratification provides causal estimands for post-treatment intermediate variables such as surrogates.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the strict-gap claim is a polytope phenomenon, not a new estimand definition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from latent-class principal-stratification bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: Standard principal-stratification bounds do not include noisy repeated measurement plus drift-cost compatibility.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChenGengJia2007 would warn that positive surrogate effects can coexist with negative endpoint effects. Our seed conjectures a sharp sign-identification boundary: the surrogate-index margin dominates all feasible drift paths if and only if the long-term ATE sign is identified.

anchor_paper.author: Chen, Geng, Jia
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series B
anchor_paper.claim: Surrogate endpoint criteria can fail through sign-discordant surrogate and true-endpoint effects.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not just deriving a sign version; the sign boundary depends on the residual drift-radius LP.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the surrogate paradox literature because it already studies sign reversals.
seed_details.rebuttal: The surrogate paradox literature gives qualitative criteria, not a sharp finite partial-ID threshold from noisy panels.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work LiLuedtke2023 would imply that efficient fusion of data sources is central once surrogate information is available. Our seed conjectures that under post-surrogate drift the efficiency question is secondary to a nondegenerate identified interval whose width is governed by drift and noisy-panel compatibility.

anchor_paper.author: Li, Luedtke
anchor_paper.claim: Data fusion can improve efficiency for finite-dimensional parameters using surrogate information across sources.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a - orthogonal to blocklist; this contrasts estimation efficiency with identification failure.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate because nonidentification always precedes efficiency analysis.
seed_details.rebuttal: The nontrivial content is the sharp width formula from drift and noisy-panel compatibility, not the slogan that ID precedes efficiency.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChenRitzwoller2023 would imply that semiparametric long-term treatment-effect estimation is feasible by combining short-term experimental and long-term observational samples. Our seed conjectures that if the observational bridge only identifies a no-drift response curve, then treated post-surrogate drift forces sharp bounds rather than a regular point estimand.

anchor_paper.author: Chen, Ritzwoller
anchor_paper.claim: Semiparametric methods estimate long-term treatment effects by combining experimental short-term data with observational long-term data.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the mechanism is treated post-surrogate drift breaking the bridge, not a generic weakening of comparability.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from failure of external validity in observational bridges.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed asks for the exact finite sharp set under a bounded drift restriction, which external-validity warnings do not provide.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Manski1990 would imply bounded-outcome intervals when the long-term outcome is unobserved. Our seed conjectures that noisy surrogate panels and drift budgets generate a strict refinement of the Manski interval through finite response-type restrictions.

anchor_paper.author: Manski
anchor_paper.claim: Nonparametric treatment-effect bounds optimize over all latent outcomes compatible with observed data and bounded outcomes.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not simply applying Manski bounds; the surprise is a strict finite-panel refinement with a measurable tightening criterion.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Manski's general optimization principle.
seed_details.rebuttal: Manski gives the principle, not the surrogate-panel drift response-type construction or strict-tightening criterion.

proposed_from.literature_map: AtheyChettyImbensKang2019 (NBER WP 26463; https://doi.org/10.3386/w26463) establishes surrogate-index identification under Prentice surrogacy and comparability, and discusses bias from violations. Prentice1989 (Statistics in Medicine) defines the operational surrogacy condition; the tension is post-surrogate drift after the last measured surrogate. FrangakisRubin2002 (Biometrics) frames post-treatment surrogate variables through principal stratification; tension is latent principal strata versus noisy repeated measurements. ChenGengJia2007 (JRSSB) gives surrogate-endpoint criteria and surrogate-paradox sign warnings; open gap is a sharp finite sign-boundary under bounded drift. ChenRitzwoller2023 (Journal of Econometrics) provides semiparametric long-term treatment-effect estimation from short-term experimental and long-term observational data; gap is partial ID when surrogacy fails. LiLuedtke2023 (Biometrika) studies efficient data fusion with surrogate information; gap is identification before efficiency under nonzero drift. BibautKallusEjdemyrZhao2023 (arXiv:2311.04657) uses many weak experiments, proxies, and cross-fold moments for imperfect surrogates; gap is what one short experiment plus noisy panel measurements can sharply identify. HuangWangYuanZhaoZhang2026 (Management Science; https://doi.org/10.1287/mnsc.2023.02575) gives a longitudinal surrogate framework for long-term treatments; tension is point-ID assumptions versus bounded-drift partial ID. LalImbensHull2026 (arXiv:2601.06359) models many noisy proxies for low-dimensional latent surrogates; open gap is nonparametric sharp bounds with treatment-induced post-surrogate drift. FanManzanaresParkQi2026 (arXiv:2603.00580) develops WSI sensitivity analysis and identified sets under unknown copulas; closest gap is terminal-surrogate copula sets versus full short-panel measurement compatibility. Manski1990 (AER) supplies nonparametric bounded-outcome partial-ID logic. [reused from pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2] BalkePearl1997, ManskiPepper2000, and DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 support the general finite-LP sharp-bounds style, but their dynamic-IV objects are not the surrogate-panel kernel here.
